slides.title: Catching Errors 🎣
slides.content:
# The try-except Block

```python
try:
    # Code that might fail
    result = 10 / 0
except:
    # Handle the error
    print("An error occurred!")
```

Catch errors instead of crashing!

slides.title: Basic try-except 💻

quizData.question: What happens when an exception is caught?
quizData.explanation: When an exception is caught, the except block runs and the program continues!

slides.title: Specific Exceptions 🎯
Multiple except 💻

quizData.question: Why catch specific exception types?
quizData.explanation: Specific exceptions let you provide appropriate handling for each error type!

slides.title: The else Clause ✅
try-except-else 💻
Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content:
## Don't Catch Everything!

```python
# BAD: Hides all errors
try:
    ...
except:  # ❌ Too broad
    pass

# GOOD: Specific exceptions
try:
    ...
except ValueError:  # ✅ Specific
    handle_error()
```

quizData.question: When does the else block in try-except run?
quizData.explanation: The else block runs only when the try block succeeds without exceptions!
